questions.question: Starting from the blue ball hopper with bit A pointing to the LEFT with the solved board configuration, describe the path of the blue ball.
questions.answer: The blue ball drops from (2,-1), rolls through the ramps and crossovers, which swap the paths to intercept a blue ball when bit A points left (reversing the expected behavior from Challenge 14).

questions.question: Starting from the blue ball hopper with bit A pointing to the RIGHT with the solved board configuration, describe the path of the balls.
questions.answer: The blue ball drops and triggers the right lever, releasing a red ball. The crossovers swap the paths so that a red ball is intercepted when bit A points right.

questions.question: How does this puzzle differ from Challenge 14, and what component makes the difference?
questions.answer: Challenge 14 intercepts blue when A is left, red when A is right. Challenge 15 is inverted (hence the title 'Inversion'). The crossover component is used to reverse the ball paths.
